one_liner: Lark (Larksuite) offers a broad REST API with tenant/app/user access tokens and OAuth2 flows, self-serve via developer console, and has an official open-source MCP server for AI agent integration.

auth_methods.notes: user_access_token obtained via OAuth-style authorization code flow for acting on behalf of a user.
tenant_access_token (t-) and app_access_token (a-/t-) are custom bearer tokens obtained via app_id/app_secret, used for server-to-server calls.

self_serve_notes: Developers can create custom apps in the Lark Developer Console, obtain app_id/app_secret, and call free/basic APIs without sales contact. Basic APIs are free up to 10,000 calls/month on Standard edition; advanced APIs require a paid plan and usage-based quota package.

api_surface.notes: Extensive server API covering Contacts, Messaging, Group Chat, Docs, Calendar, Video Conferencing, Approval, Attendance, Tasks, Email, Search, AI, Admin, OKR, and more, documented at open.larksuite.com.

main_blocker: None significant for basic use; advanced API tiers require a paid Lark plan and purchased quota package, which could gate some functionality behind admin/paid approval.

evidence.supports: Describes tenant_access_token, app_access_token, user_access_token and how to obtain them for API auth.
Details free/basic/advanced API tiers, free 10,000 calls/month limit on Standard edition, and paid quota packages for advanced APIs.
Documents OAuth-style authorization code flow for obtaining user_access_token.
Lists official Lark OpenAPI MCP tool (@larksuiteoapi/lark-mcp) enabling AI agents to connect via stdio/HTTP/SSE.

flags: Advanced API tier requires paid plan + purchased quota, may limit some agent capabilities on free tier.
Official MCP server exists but community alternatives (e.g., Lark Master MCP) also referenced, suggesting coverage gaps in official tool.
